description:
## Goal

For constant byte-literal binaries above the shared-zone threshold, bake the `SharedBin` struct itself into the module's `.rodata` rather than allocating it at startup. Runtime construction becomes: one ProcBin alloc on the heap pointing at the static SharedBin. The bytes never get copied at any point in the run.

## Depends on

fz-cty.8 (constant-fold MakeBitstring).

## Design

- New layout-compatible static-SharedBin variant or sentinel: refcount initialised to `usize::MAX / 2` (or a dedicated bit pattern) that `shared_bin_retain` no-ops on (saturating add) and `shared_bin_release` no-ops on (saturating sub). Never reaches zero, never freed.
- Codegen for above-threshold `Prim::ConstBitstring`: emit a static SharedBin in `.rodata` with the bytes inline; emit one heap-side ProcBin allocation at runtime.
- Runtime: `shared_bin_retain` / `release` recognise the sentinel and skip the atomic op. (Or use the standard atomic ops and rely on the count never approaching zero.)

## Non-changes

- Below-threshold constants stay inline via the existing `Heap::alloc_bitstring` path.
- Runtime-constructed bitstrings unaffected.

## Open questions

- Layout: SharedBin currently uses `Box<[u8]>` for the byte payload. A static SharedBin would either need a flexible-array-member layout (DST) or a thin-pointer-to-static-slice variant. DST is more uniform but requires unsafe layout; thin pointer is simpler but adds an indirection per byte access (probably negligible).
- Whether to file separately or fold into fz-cty.8: keeping it separate so .8 lands quickly and this is opt-in if the static path proves worth the complexity.
title: fz-cty.9: static SharedBin in .rodata for above-threshold constants (follow-up)
